reason:
Both answers converge on the same core recommendation (contractor to stabilize + migrate to managed k8s, avoid a single replacement hire) and back it up with similar reasoning on cost, bus-factor, and migration risk. Neither explicitly raises the "consolidate/simplify the 40 services" angle, so both miss that key point equally.

Differences are minor and roughly offsetting: Answer B adds a concrete "this week" self-serve step (audit access/secrets/backups, verify someone else can deploy) before the contractor even starts, which is a genuinely useful immediate-risk-reduction detail. Answer A adds an explicit caution against over-rotating to a full replatform (ECS/Cloud Run/Nomad) during the crisis, which is also a useful guardrail. Both are correct, concise, and actionable, landing on essentially the same plan with comparable coverage of the key points.
